questions.question: Explain why health is considered a balance between physical, mental, and social well-being rather than just the absence of disease.
questions.answer: Health involves proper body functioning, mental stability, and positive relationships. Even if a person is free from disease, stress, loneliness, or poor social interaction can affect overall well-being.

questions.question: Analyze how excessive screen time can affect both physical and mental health.
questions.answer: Excessive screen time can lead to eye strain, obesity, sleep disturbances, and reduced social interaction. It can also increase stress and anxiety.

questions.question: Evaluate the importance of a balanced lifestyle in preventing diseases.
questions.answer: A balanced lifestyle including nutritious food, exercise, proper sleep, and stress management helps maintain immunity and reduces the risk of many diseases.

questions.question: Discuss the relationship between environmental conditions and the spread of diseases.
questions.answer: Poor sanitation, polluted water, and unclean surroundings create conditions for pathogens to multiply and spread diseases.

questions.question: Analyze how urban pollution can affect respiratory health.
questions.answer: Air pollution from vehicles and industries introduces harmful particles that irritate the respiratory system and may cause asthma or breathing problems.

questions.question: Explain how symptoms and signs help doctors diagnose diseases.
questions.answer: Symptoms describe how a patient feels while signs are observable indicators. Together they help doctors identify diseases accurately.

questions.question: Analyze the difference between communicable and non-communicable diseases with suitable examples.
questions.answer: Communicable diseases spread through pathogens while non-communicable diseases are caused by lifestyle or environmental factors.

questions.question: Explain the role of pathogens in causing infectious diseases.
questions.answer: Pathogens such as bacteria, viruses, fungi, and protozoa invade the body, multiply, and interfere with normal body functions causing diseases.

questions.question: Discuss the different modes through which communicable diseases spread.
questions.answer: Diseases spread through air droplets, contaminated water and food, direct contact, and vectors such as mosquitoes.

questions.question: Evaluate the impact of mosquito breeding in stagnant water on community health.
questions.answer: Stagnant water allows mosquitoes to breed and increases the risk of diseases such as malaria and dengue.

questions.question: Explain how improved sanitation can reduce the spread of communicable diseases.
questions.answer: Sanitation prevents contamination of water and food sources and reduces the transmission of pathogens.

questions.question: Analyze why non-communicable diseases are increasing in modern society.
questions.answer: Changes in lifestyle, increased consumption of processed food, lack of exercise, and longer life expectancy contribute to the rise of non-communicable diseases.

questions.question: Explain how lifestyle factors contribute to diseases like diabetes and obesity.
questions.answer: Poor diet, lack of physical activity, and excessive sugar consumption increase the risk of diabetes and obesity.

questions.question: Describe the concept of immunity and its role in protecting the body from infections.
questions.answer: Immunity is the body's natural defense mechanism that identifies and destroys pathogens.

questions.question: Explain how vaccines help the immune system recognize pathogens.
questions.answer: Vaccines introduce harmless forms of pathogens, enabling the immune system to recognize them and respond quickly during future infections.

questions.question: Analyze the significance of vaccination programs in controlling infectious diseases.
questions.answer: Vaccination programs prevent the spread of diseases by building immunity in large populations.

questions.question: Explain how Edward Jenner’s experiment contributed to modern vaccination.
questions.answer: Edward Jenner discovered that exposure to cowpox protected against smallpox, leading to the development of the first vaccine.

questions.question: Evaluate India's role in global vaccine production.
questions.answer: India is one of the largest producers of vaccines and supplies them to many countries worldwide.

questions.question: Explain how antibiotics help in treating bacterial infections.
questions.answer: Antibiotics kill bacteria or prevent them from multiplying, helping cure bacterial infections.

questions.question: Analyze why antibiotics are ineffective against viral infections.
questions.answer: Antibiotics target bacterial structures and cannot affect viruses because viruses have different biological structures.

questions.question: Explain the concept of antibiotic resistance and how it develops.
questions.answer: Antibiotic resistance occurs when bacteria evolve to survive antibiotic treatments due to overuse or misuse of antibiotics.

questions.question: Discuss how misuse of antibiotics can affect future medical treatments.
questions.answer: Misuse of antibiotics allows bacteria to develop resistance, making infections more difficult to treat.

questions.question: Explain how emotional stress can affect the immune system.
questions.answer: Stress weakens the immune system and increases susceptibility to diseases.

questions.question: Discuss the importance of community awareness programs in preventing diseases.
questions.answer: Community awareness programs educate people about hygiene, vaccination, and sanitation, reducing disease spread.

questions.question: Evaluate the combined role of hygiene, vaccination, and healthy lifestyle in maintaining good health.
questions.answer: Hygiene prevents pathogen spread, vaccination builds immunity, and a healthy lifestyle strengthens the body, together ensuring overall health.
